steps.title: Find the radius
steps.content:
The base area is $324\pi$, and the area of a circular base is $\pi r^2$. So
$$
\pi r^2=324\pi
$$
$$
r=18
$$

steps.title: Find the height
steps.content:
Use the cone volume formula:
$$
2{,}592\pi=\frac{1}{3}\pi r^2h
$$
Since $r^2=324$,
$$
2{,}592\pi=108\pi h
$$
$$
h=24
$$

steps.title: Find the slant height
steps.content: The radius, height, and slant height form a right triangle: $$ \ell^2=18^2+24^2=324+576=900 $$ $$ \ell=30 $$ The answer is D.
